application.name: Cypress Real World App
application.description: A payment application demonstrating real-world usage of Cypress testing methods

testScenarios.title: User Sign Up - Valid Registration

steps.expected: Sign Up form is displayed
First name field accepts input
Last name field accepts input
Username field accepts input
Password field accepts input (masked)
Confirm password field accepts input (masked)
User is redirected to Sign In page with success message

testScenarios.expectedResult: User account is created successfully and redirected to Sign In page

testScenarios.title: User Sign In - Valid Credentials
preconditions: User is on Sign In page

steps.expected: Username field accepts input
Password field accepts input (masked)
User is redirected to home page with transaction feed

testScenarios.expectedResult: User is successfully logged in and redirected to home page

testScenarios.title: User Sign In - Invalid Credentials
preconditions: User is on Sign In page

steps.expected: Username field accepts input
Password field accepts input (masked)
Error message is displayed indicating invalid credentials

testScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails and error message is shown

testScenarios.title: User Sign Out
preconditions: User is on any page of the application

steps.action: Click on navigation menu (hamburger icon for mobile)
steps.expected: Navigation drawer opens
User is redirected to Sign In page

testScenarios.expectedResult: User is successfully logged out and redirected to Sign In page

testScenarios.title: Create Payment Transaction - Valid Data
preconditions: User has contacts in the system

steps.expected: New transaction form opens - Step 1: Select Contact

steps.action: Search for contact by typing first name in search field
steps.expected: Contact list is filtered based on search criteria

steps.action: Select a contact from the list
steps.expected: Contact is selected and form proceeds to Step 2: Payment
Amount field accepts valid monetary input
Description field accepts input
Transaction is processed and Step 3: Complete is shown
Success message shows 'Paid $25.00 for Lunch payment'

testScenarios.expectedResult: Payment transaction is created successfully and user balance is updated

testScenarios.title: Create Request Transaction - Valid Data
preconditions: User has contacts in the system

steps.expected: New transaction form opens - Step 1: Select Contact
Contact is selected and form proceeds to Step 2: Payment
Amount field accepts valid monetary input
Description field accepts input
Transaction request is processed and Step 3: Complete is shown
Success message shows 'Requested $50.00 for Dinner split'

testScenarios.expectedResult: Request transaction is created successfully and notification is sent to contact

testScenarios.title: View Transaction Details

steps.expected: Personal transactions list is displayed
Transaction detail page opens
All transaction details are shown: amount, description, participants, date
Social interaction counts are displayed

testScenarios.expectedResult: Transaction details are displayed correctly with all relevant information

testScenarios.title: Like a Transaction
preconditions: User is viewing a transaction detail page
Transaction has not been liked by current user

steps.action: Click the like button (thumbs up icon)
steps.expected: Like button is disabled and like count increases by 1
Like count reflects the new like

testScenarios.expectedResult: Transaction is liked successfully and like count is updated

testScenarios.title: Comment on Transaction
preconditions: User is viewing a transaction detail page

steps.expected: Comment form is visible
Comment text is entered in the field
Comment is submitted and appears in comments list

testScenarios.expectedResult: Comment is posted successfully and visible in transaction comments

testScenarios.title: Accept Transaction Request
preconditions: User has received a transaction request
Transaction request is in pending status

steps.expected: Personal transactions list is displayed
Transaction detail page opens with Accept/Reject buttons
Transaction is accepted and buttons are hidden

testScenarios.expectedResult: Transaction request is accepted and status is updated to complete

testScenarios.title: Reject Transaction Request
preconditions: User has received a transaction request
Transaction request is in pending status

steps.expected: Personal transactions list is displayed
Transaction detail page opens with Accept/Reject buttons
Transaction is rejected and buttons are hidden

testScenarios.expectedResult: Transaction request is rejected and status is updated to rejected

testScenarios.title: Transaction Form Validation - Empty Fields
preconditions: User is on Step 2 of new transaction form

steps.expected: Amount field is empty
Description field is empty
Validation errors are displayed for required fields

testScenarios.expectedResult: Form validation prevents submission with empty required fields

testScenarios.title: Transaction Form Validation - Invalid Amount
preconditions: User is on Step 2 of new transaction form

steps.expected: Amount field accepts input
Description field accepts input
Validation error for invalid amount is displayed

testScenarios.expectedResult: Form validation prevents submission with invalid amount

testScenarios.title: Add Bank Account - Valid Data
preconditions: User navigates to Bank Accounts page

steps.expected: Bank account form is displayed
Bank name field accepts input
Account number field accepts input
Routing number field accepts input
Bank account is created and appears in the list

testScenarios.expectedResult: Bank account is successfully created and displayed in the list

testScenarios.title: Delete Bank Account
preconditions: User has at least one bank account
User is on Bank Accounts page

steps.action: Click 'Delete' button next to a bank account
steps.expected: Confirmation dialog or bank account is immediately deleted
Bank account is removed from the list

testScenarios.expectedResult: Bank account is successfully deleted and removed from the list

testScenarios.title: Bank Account Form Validation - Empty Fields
preconditions: User is on bank account creation form

steps.expected: All fields are empty
Validation errors are displayed for required fields

testScenarios.expectedResult: Form validation prevents submission with empty required fields

testScenarios.title: Bank Accounts API - Full CRUD Operations Manual Test
preconditions: API testing tool available (Postman, curl, or browser dev tools)

steps.action: SETUP: Login via API to get authentication token
steps.expected: Receive 200 status and authentication token in response

steps.action: TEST: GET /api/bankAccounts - Retrieve user's bank accounts
steps.details: Send GET request to /api/bankAccounts with Authorization header
steps.expected: Status: 200, Response contains array of bank accounts for authenticated user

steps.action: VERIFY: Check response structure for GET bank accounts
steps.details: Validate response has 'results' array and first item has 'userId' matching authenticated user
steps.expected: Response structure is correct and userId matches authenticated user

steps.action: TEST: POST /api/bankAccounts - Create new bank account
steps.details: Send POST request with body: {bankName: 'Test Bank Corp', accountNumber: '1234567890', routingNumber: '987654321'}
steps.expected: Status: 200, Response contains newly created bank account with generated ID

steps.action: VERIFY: Check created bank account response
steps.details: Validate response has 'account' object with 'id' (string) and 'userId' matching authenticated user
steps.expected: Created bank account has valid structure and correct userId

steps.action: STORE: Save created bank account ID for subsequent tests
steps.details: Extract and store the 'id' field from the created bank account for use in GET and DELETE tests
steps.expected: Bank account ID is captured for further testing

steps.action: TEST: GET /api/bankAccounts/{id} - Retrieve specific bank account
steps.details: Send GET request to /api/bankAccounts/{bankAccountId} using ID from step 6
steps.expected: Status: 200, Response contains the specific bank account details

steps.action: VERIFY: Check individual bank account response
steps.details: Validate response has 'account' object with correct userId and matching the created account details
steps.expected: Retrieved bank account matches the created account and belongs to authenticated user

steps.action: TEST: GraphQL Query - List bank accounts
steps.details: Send POST to /api/graphql with query: 'query { listBankAccount { id uuid userId bankName accountNumber routingNumber isDeleted createdAt modifiedAt } }'
steps.expected: Status: 200, Response contains GraphQL data with bank accounts array

steps.details: Validate response has 'data.listBankAccount' array with no errors, first item has userId matching authenticated user
steps.expected: GraphQL response is properly formatted and contains user's bank accounts

steps.action: TEST: GraphQL Mutation - Create bank account
steps.details: Send POST to /api/graphql with mutation: 'mutation createBankAccount($bankName: String!, $accountNumber: String!, $routingNumber: String!) { createBankAccount(bankName: $bankName, accountNumber: $accountNumber, routingNumber: $routingNumber) { id uuid userId bankName accountNumber routingNumber isDeleted createdAt } }' and variables
steps.expected: Status: 200, Response contains created bank account via GraphQL

steps.details: Validate response has 'data.createBankAccount' with userId matching authenticated user
steps.expected: Bank account created via GraphQL has correct userId

steps.details: Send DELETE request to /api/bankAccounts/{bankAccountId} using ID from step 6
steps.expected: Status: 200, Bank account is successfully deleted

steps.action: TEST: GraphQL Mutation - Delete bank account
steps.details: Send POST to /api/graphql with mutation: 'mutation deleteBankAccount($id: ID!) { deleteBankAccount(id: $id) }' using a valid bank account ID
steps.expected: Status: 200, Bank account is successfully deleted via GraphQL

steps.details: Send GET request to /api/bankAccounts to verify the deleted accounts are no longer present
steps.expected: Deleted bank accounts are not returned in the list

testScenarios.expectedResult: All bank account API endpoints (REST and GraphQL) function correctly: GET returns user's accounts, POST creates new accounts, DELETE removes accounts, and GraphQL queries/mutations work as expected
notes: This test covers the same scenarios as the automated api-bankaccounts.spec.ts test
Requires API testing tool (Postman, curl, or browser dev tools)
Authentication token must be obtained and included in all requests
Test data should use realistic bank names and account numbers
GraphQL mutations should include proper variable binding
Error handling can be tested by using invalid IDs or missing authentication

apiEndpoints.description: Get all bank accounts for authenticated user
Get specific bank account by ID
GraphQL endpoint for bank account operations

bodyExample.query: query { listBankAccount { id userId bankName accountNumber routingNumber } }

testScenarios.title: Bank Transfer - Insufficient Funds
preconditions: User has insufficient balance for transaction

steps.action: Attempt to create payment exceeding current balance
steps.expected: Transaction is processed

steps.action: Check if bank transfer withdrawal is created
steps.expected: Bank transfer withdrawal covers the deficit amount

steps.action: Verify user balance is reset to zero
steps.expected: User balance shows $0.00

testScenarios.expectedResult: System handles insufficient funds by creating bank transfer withdrawal
